title: salix humilis var. humilis
intro: Salix humilis var. humilis, also known as the prairie willow or low willow, is a small, deciduous shrub or tree belonging to the willow family (Salicaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Salix humilis var. humilis include reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into salves, ointments, and tinctures to be used topically or taken internally, but it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
